blocks.content: Six months ago. The night of the carnival.

Ethan sat in his car in the parking lot of his apartment building for a long time after he got home. Not because he couldn't go inside. Just because the car felt like a place where he was allowed to sit with everything that had happened before he carried it through a door.
Me too. Well. Lena's couch. Same concept.
A pause. Three dots. They disappeared. Then came back.
I feel like I put something down that I've been carrying for a very long time and my arms don't know what to do yet.
He looked out through the windshield at the apartment building. The lights in the windows. People going about their evenings, ordinary and unknowing.
I'm okay. Not the pretending kind. The real kind.
I've been sitting here thinking about what I want to say and I keep not finding the right words so I'm going to say the true ones instead.
